probes.name: user-tables-migrated
probes.description: The `users`, `profiles`, and `tenant_members` tables exist + carry the latest schema version. Failure indicates a missed migration.

probes.name: user-identity-fk-integrity
probes.description: Every `users.identity_id` resolves to a live `identities` row. Failure indicates orphaned rows after an out-of-band identity purge.
probes.reference: The FK is onDelete=restrict so the DB should never permit orphans; this probe catches direct SQL manipulation.

probes.name: user-profile-1to1-integrity
probes.description: No `users.profile_id` is null. No two Users share a `profile_id`. Runs a limited-cost query with a covering index scan.

probes.name: user-default-tenant-invariant
probes.description: Every User with at least one TenantMember has EXACTLY ONE row with `is_default = true`. Failure is a data-quality signal; the observer + partial unique index normally prevents this state.

probes.name: user-purge-queue-drained
probes.description: No `PurgeErasedUsersJob` has been pending for > 24h. Catches GDPR-relevant queue stalls that could push a soft-deleted row past its legal retention limit.

probes.name: user-sanctum-tokens-reachable
probes.description: The `personal_access_tokens` table is queryable + the Sanctum guard resolves cleanly. Non-critical because a broken Sanctum path is caught at auth-time regardless.
